raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 5,
    "R1.7": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The agent audits the evidence-claim alignment by identifying three internal contradictions between the stated goal, the benchmark, and the novelty claim.",
    "R1.7": "The agent flags one missing or wrong citation.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the novelty claim is contradicted by the very citations that support it).",
    "RX.3": "The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as the agent provides critique and suggestions for improvement instead of evaluating the original text.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (overreaching novelty claim).",
    "RX.5": "Major rewrites are traced; minor edits are not."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.8,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The agent audits the evidence-claim alignment by identifying three internal contradictions between the stated goal, the benchmark, and the novelty claim.
rationale.R1.7: The agent flags one missing or wrong citation.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the novelty claim is contradicted by the very citations that support it).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as the agent provides critique and suggestions for improvement instead of evaluating the original text.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (overreaching novelty claim).
rationale.RX.5: Major rewrites are traced; minor edits are not.
